summary: Scorching Jerry solos, stellar Bird Song & tight China>Rider
blurb: Jerry delivers scorching solos on In The Midnight Hour and He's Gone while the band locks in with a powerful Bird Song and seamless China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition. Highlights include Desolation Row, Cold Rain And Snow, and an energetic second set that rolls into Black Peter and Good Lovin'.
review:
On March 26, 1987 at the Hartford Civic Center the Dead turned in a high‑energy performance that still shines for spring ’87 fans. Garcia was on fire – his solo on In The Midnight Hour was blistering, he hit soaring notes during He's Gone and delivered a monster Bird Song that had the house roaring. The rhythm section stayed tight; Phil’s bass underpinned the groove while both drummers drove the jams with punchy toms and steady back‑beat. Brent’s piano added jazzy fills that lifted the Bird Song jam, and Bob contributed rock‑n‑roll rhythm guitar on The Promised Land.

The setlist was packed with classics performed at peak intensity: a flawless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue, a soulful Cold Rain And Snow, an inspired Desolation Row, and a swinging Black Peter that flowed into Good Lovin'. The encore’s The Mighty Quinn closed the night on a high note. Across multiple sources the show is praised for its tight ensemble play, standout jams, and memorable vocal moments, making this recording a must‑listen for anyone tracking the band’s spring ’87 surge.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Seven of nine recordings describe the show in positive terms, highlighting Jerry’s scorching solos, strong Bird Song, and tight ensemble play; only one mixed and one negative review note minor issues (first‑set speed problems, rushed Bob). The overwhelming consensus is that the performance is high‑energy and memorable, justifying a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear audio, preserving the high‑energy performance and all standout jams (He's Gone, Bird Song, China Cat Sunflower).

key_highlights: Garcia’s scorching solo on In The Midnight Hour and soaring notes on He's Gone
Monster Bird Song that drives the second set with crowd chanting
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition
Tight rhythm section delivering powerful Desolation Row and Black Peter

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia was on fire all night – scorching solo tones, high‑note thrills on He's Gone, a monster Bird Song and strong vocals that lifted every jam.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provided a rock‑solid bass foundation throughout; his playful fist‑shake moment added extra focus during the second set.
band_performance.Bob: Weir supplied solid rhythm guitar work, notably trading riffs with Jerry on The Promised Land, though one reviewer noted he sounded a bit rushed in places.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s piano offered jazzy fills that complemented the Bird Song jam and kept the harmonic texture lively across the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart's drumming added powerful high‑end toms and a strong rhythmic backbone, especially evident during the intense first half.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann delivered tight drum interplay with Mickey, driving the energy forward and highlighting transitions like the Space → I Need A Miracle flow.
